hypothesis: If a conditional latent diffusion model is trained in the latent space of a chemically-informed autoencoder (CIAE) and conditioned on adsorbate identity and adsorption energy, then it will generate shell-wise catalytic surface composition vectors that, compared to chemically-agnostic baselines, exhibit lower Fréchet Distance to the true distribution, higher compliance with allowed-element constraints (lower Leakage), greater cosine similarity to target compositions, and improved data efficiency when pretrained on OC20 and fine-tuned on HEA.
